message:
perf(vdbe): implement StorageCursor reuse via Rc refill across VDBE executions

The highest-impact remaining optimization: retain StorageCursors across
repeated VDBE executions instead of recreating them each time (~500-1000ns
per row eliminated).

Previously, each VDBE execution called set_transaction() which created a
new Rc<RefCell<TransactionKind>>, making all retained cursor Rc refs
invalid. Now:

1. SharedTxnPageIo::refill() swaps the inner TransactionKind without
   breaking Rc references held by retained cursors.

2. SharedTxnPageIo::drain() extracts the txn while keeping the Rc alive
   (inner becomes TransactionKind::Drained sentinel).

3. VdbeEngine gains refill_transaction/drain_transaction/has_txn_page_io
   public methods for the connection to orchestrate cursor retention.

4. execute_table_program_with_db now calls reset_for_reuse_ex with
   retain_cursors=true when reusing a cached engine with an existing
   txn_page_io. Uses refill_transaction instead of set_transaction, and
   drain_transaction instead of take_transaction.

5. TransactionKind gains a Drained variant that panics on access — it
   only exists transiently between drain and the next refill.

This means OpenWrite detects existing cursors on the same root page and
reuses them (buffers, B-tree stack allocations preserved), skipping the
full cursor creation ceremony.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
